---
title: "Semiring"
generated: true
description: "The relaxed semiring: a frozen bundle of the algebraic operations."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.semiring`](../nitrix-semiring.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.semiring._types`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Semiring -->
```python
class Semiring(Generic[S])
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:Semiring -->
The relaxed semiring: a frozen bundle of the algebraic operations.

A semiring pairs a [`Monoid`](../nitrix-semiring/Monoid.qmd) (the additive reduction `(+)`)
with a [`Semigroup`](../nitrix-semiring/Semigroup.qmd) (the multiplicative combine `(*)`), plus
the identity/annihilator scalars and the optional differentiation
rules needed by the streaming kernels. The relaxed variant makes no
associativity promise on `binary_op`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Semiring.as_strict -->
```python
as_strict() -> 'StrictSemiring[S]'
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Re-tag this semiring as a [`StrictSemiring`](../nitrix-semiring/StrictSemiring.qmd).

The caller is asserting that `binary_op` is associative and
distributes over `monoid.merge` for the dtypes in use. No check
is performed; the type system carries the obligation.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Semiring.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(monoid: Monoid[S], binary_op: Semigroup, identity: Any = None, annihilator: Any = None, name: str = 'semiring', matmul_vjp: Optional[SemiringMatmulVJP] = None, ell_matmul_vjp: Optional[SemiringELLMatmulVJP] = None) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:Semiring -->
::: {.callout-note}
Construct via `Semiring(...)` for the relaxed variant or
`StrictSemiring(...)` directly for the strict variant.
`Semiring.as_strict` and `StrictSemiring.as_relaxed`
convert between them; the conversion is a re-tagging, not a check.

User-defined algebras may subclass this dataclass only to attach
extra metadata (e.g., a custom gradient hint); the kernel surface
relies on the `monoid` / `binary_op` / `identity` / `name`
fields.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
